macro_segments.title: The Status of Samaritans (Kutim) in Goring Law and the Principle of Knas

micro_segments.title: Mishna: Ox of Jew gores Samaritan vs. Samaritan ox gores Jew
Chachamim: Half/Full Payment Based on Tam/Muad; Interpretation as Knas for Separation
Rabbi Meir: Full Payment Even as Tam—Samaritans as Gerei Arayos (Converts Due to Lions)
Challenge: Blood Stains (Ktzamim) from Rekem—Rabbi Meir and Chachamim Agree Kutim are Halachically Jewish
Resolution: Rabbi Meir Imposes Knas on Samaritans' Money Regarding Goring; Distinguishing Goring from Seduction Cases
Abaye: Why No Knas for Seduction—Wrongdoer Must Face Consequences; Rav Mari: Unprosecutable Debt

macro_segments.title: Goring by Ox of Cheresh, Shoteh, and Katan—Appointment of Apotropus

micro_segments.title: Mishna: Normal Person's Ox Gores Those Lacking Full Intellect (Cheresh, Shoteh, Katan) = Liable; Reverse Exempt
Appointment of Apotropus as Custodian; Testimony Before Apotropus to Make Ox Muad
Cheresh Recovers, Shoteh Recovers, Katan Matures—Ox Reverts to Tam? Rabbi Meir vs. Rabbi Yosi (Reshus Meshaneh)
Yi'udei Tora (Nature of Ox) vs. Yi'udei Gavra (Owner's Liability)—Interpretive Framework
Gemaric Analysis: Internal Contradiction in Mishna—Payment Only from Muad, Not Tam
Rava's Reading: If Ox is a Nagach, Apotropus Appointed; Testimony Makes It Muad; Payment After Muad Status

macro_segments.title: Payment Liability of Apotropus vs. Minor Orphans—Incentive and Ongoing Loss

micro_segments.title: Who Pays After Harm—Orphans vs. Apotropus? Rabbi Yochanan vs. Rabbi Yosi bar Chanina
Objection: Rabbi Yochanan's Principle Protects Orphan Property While Minors
Resolution: Mazik (Damaging Entity) is Distinct—Different Category Justifying Payment from Orphans
Competing Incentive Logic: Apotropus Needs Exemption from Liability vs. Later Recovery from Orphans
Tosafot Distinction: Two Types of Apotropus—Community Policeman (No Status) vs. Estate Trustee (High Status & Liability)

macro_segments.title: Tanaim Debate on Apotropus Authority—Payment Collection Before Muad Status

micro_segments.title: Sumchus vs. Chachamim: Does Apotropus Allow Collection from Tam's Body?
Gemaric Clarification: Sumchus's 'Remaining in Temimus' = Remains Complete Tam; No Payment as Tam
Seifa Debate: Reshus Meshaneh—Does Change of Ownership Revert Muad to Tam?

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yaakov on Payment from Cheresh, Shoteh, Katan Ox—Half Damages (Chatzi Nezek)

micro_segments.title: Braiта: Rabbi Yaakov Says Half Damages for Ox of Cheresh, Shoteh, Katan
Gemaric Question: If Tam, Obviously Half; If Muad, Should Be Full or Exempt—Why Say Half?
Rava: Case of Muad with Intermediate (B-Level) Shmirah—Holds Like Rabbi Yehuda's Tzad Tam Bimkomo
Rabbi Yehuda's Doctrine: Muad Needs Only B-Level Shmirah to be Exempt; Tam Needs A-Level Shmirah
Rabbi Yaakov's Synthesis: Half-Tam Half-Muad Ox; B-Level Shmirah Exempts Muad Portion, Leaves Tam Liable for Half

Rabbi Yosi / Rabbi Yosi bar Chanina
